stem: How many integers are in the domain of \( f(x) = \sqrt{x - ([[a]])} + \sqrt{([[b]]) - x} \)?
solution: Both radicands must be nonnegative: \( x - ([[a]]) \ge 0 \) and \( ([[b]]) - x \ge 0 \), giving \( [[a]] \le x \le [[b]] \). This closed interval contains \( [[b]] - [[a]] + 1 = [[answer]] \) integers.
distractor_rationales: This is the interval length \( b - a \); counting integers in a closed interval adds 1.
This over-counts by one beyond the correct integer count.
This treats the endpoints as excluded, but the radicals allow equality.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.

stem: How many integers \( x \) with \( [[a]] \le x \le [[U]] \) lie in the domain of \( f(x) = \dfrac{\sqrt{x - ([[a]])}}{x - ([[excl]])} \)?
solution: The radical requires \( x \ge [[a]] \), and the denominator excludes the single point \( x = [[excl]] \), which lies inside \( [[a]], [[U]] \). Of the \( [[U]] - [[a]] + 1 \) integers in \( [[a]], [[U]] \), removing that one excluded point leaves \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to remove the point where the denominator vanishes.
This under-counts the window by one before removing the excluded point.
This removes the excluded point but also drops an extra integer from the window.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.

stem: How many integers are in the domain of \( f(x) = \log(x - ([[a]])) + \sqrt{([[b]]) - x} \)?
solution: The logarithm requires \( x - ([[a]]) > 0 \), a STRICT inequality, while the radical only requires \( ([[b]]) - x \ge 0 \). So the domain is the half-open interval \( ([[a]], [[b]]] \), which contains \( [[b]] - ([[a]]) = [[answer]] \) integers (excluding \( [[a]] \) but including \( [[b]] \)).
distractor_rationales: This treats \( [[a]] \) as included, but the logarithm's strict inequality excludes it.
This also excludes \( [[b]] \), but the radical allows equality there.
This treats both endpoints as included, ignoring the logarithm's strict inequality.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.

stem: How many integers \( x \) with \( [[L]] \le x \le [[U]] \) lie in the domain of \( f(x) = \dfrac{1}{(x - ([[p]]))(x - ([[q]]))} \)?
solution: The denominator vanishes at the two points \( x = [[p]] \) and \( x = [[q]] \), both of which lie in \( [[L]], [[U]] \). Removing these 2 excluded points from the \( [[U]] - [[L]] + 1 \) integers in the window leaves \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to remove either excluded point.
This removes only one of the two excluded points.
This removes an extra point that was never excluded.
This is larger than the correct count.
This is smaller than the correct count.
This is larger than the correct count.
